title: Bioreciclaje de CÃ¡diz and EnagÃ¡s Renovable Join Forces With Waga Energy to Produce Biomethane at the Medina Sidonia Controlled Landfill
content:
Bioreciclaje de CÃ¡diz, a public-private consortium formed by Valoriza, GS Inima, and the CÃ¡diz Waste Consortium, has partnered with EnagÃ¡s Renovable and Waga Energy to develop a biomethane production plant at the Medina Sidonia Controlled Landfill in Andalusia. Under the agreement, Waga Energy will install and operate its patented WAGABOXÂ® technology to upgrade landfill biogas into biomethane. The facility, expected to begin operations in 2027, will recover up to 2,400 mÂ³/h of biogas and inject around 80 GWh of biomethane annually into Spainâ€™s natural gas gridâ€”equivalent to the yearly consumption of 16,000 homes.

The project will cut approximately 21,000 tons of COâ‚‚ equivalent emissions each year by replacing fossil gas with renewable gas, advancing Spainâ€™s climate goals and circular economy. Company leaders emphasized the importance of the collaboration, highlighting sustainability, innovation, and energy independence. Located at the Miramundo-Los Hardales Environmental Complex, the initiative enhances existing waste-to-energy operations and positions CÃ¡diz as a benchmark in renewable energy and sustainable waste management in Southern Europe.
